title: Palindromic Factors
description: You are given a positive integer $A$. Find the number of factors of $A$ which are palindromes. A number is called a palindrome if it remains the same when the digits in decimal representation are reversed. For instance, 121 is a palindrome, while 123 is not.
input:
The first line of the input gives the number of test cases, $T$. $T$ lines follow.

Each line represents a test case and contains a single integer $A$.
output: For each test case, output one line containing Case #x: y, where $x$ is the test case number (starting from 1) and $y$ is the number of factors of $A$ which are palindromes.
hint:
In the first test case, $A$ has $4$ factors which are palindromes: $1$, $2$, $3$, and $6$.

In the second test case, $A$ has $3$ factors which are palindromes: $1$, $2$, and $5$.

In the third test case, $A$ has $7$ factors which are palindromes: $1$, $2$, $3$, $4$, $6$, $8$, and $9$.

In the fourth test case, $A$ has $6$ factors which are palindromes: $1$, $2$, $11$, $22$, $121$, and $242$.
source: Contest, Google, Kick Start, Google Kick Start 2022, Round B
